label: Chili Piper
description: Route and book inbound meetings instantly using Chili Piper's smart scheduling and lead-routing engine.

form.title: Connect Chili Piper
form.subtitle: Paste your Chili Piper API key to give Anton access to your meeting routing and booking data.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste an API key from Chili Piper's Admin panel. The simplest and fastest way to connect — no OAuth app setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Chili Piper API Key

Chili Piper issues API keys from the Admin portal. You'll need an admin account to generate one.

### Steps

1. Log in to your Chili Piper account at [app.chilipiper.com](https://app.chilipiper.com).
2. Click your workspace name or avatar in the top-right corner to open the account menu.
3. Navigate to **Admin → API**.
4. If no key exists, click **Generate API Key** (or **Create New Key**).
5. Copy the key — it may only be shown once, so store it somewhere safe.
6. Paste it into the **API Key** field below.
7. Enter your Chili Piper **workspace subdomain** (e.g. `acme` if your URL is `acme.chilipiper.com`).

### Notes

- Only workspace admins can view or generate API keys.
- If you lose the key, regenerate it from the same Admin → API page; the old key will be invalidated.
- Keep the key secret — it grants full API access to your workspace.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Found in Chili Piper Admin → API. Shown once on generation — copy and store it securely.

fields.name: workspace
fields.label: Workspace Subdomain
fields.description: Your Chili Piper subdomain — the part before .chilipiper.com in your workspace URL.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (BYOK)
methods.description: Authorize via OAuth using your own Chili Piper OAuth client credentials. More setup required but allows token-based access without a static API key.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own Client)

This method uses OAuth 2.0 to authenticate. You must create an OAuth application in Chili Piper's developer settings first.

### Steps

1. Log in to [app.chilipiper.com](https://app.chilipiper.com) as an admin.
2. Go to **Admin → Integrations → Developer / OAuth Apps**.
3. Click **Create OAuth App** and fill in a name (e.g. `Anton`) and a redirect URI of `http://127.0.0.1` (or the redirect Anton instructs you to use).
4. After saving, copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** shown on the app detail page.
5. Paste both values into the fields below and click **Submit**.
6. A browser window will open to Chili Piper's consent screen — approve the requested permissions.

### Notes

- The client secret is shown only once. Store it securely before closing the page.
- If you need to rotate credentials, create a new OAuth app and update both fields in Anton.
- Consult [Chili Piper's developer docs](https://developer.chilipiper.com) for scope details.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From your OAuth app in Chili Piper Admin → Developer / OAuth Apps.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID. Shown once when the OAuth app is created.
